entry.title: Automate User Lifecycle Management [SAP IAS]: Block or Delete with Confidence
entry.content:
Common Use case across IT Landscape :
Users who have not logged into SAP applications for the last 90 days to be automatically set to Block status.  IT administrators and application owners to easily identify inactive or unused accounts, improving security visibility and control.
 
Introducing this feature for SAP IAS - Tenant administrator can now configure specific user types to be blocked and deleted, or only blocked, or only deleted after a certain period of inactivity.
 
The users for which blocking can be enabled are of the type:
  • Public
  • Customers
  • Partners
  • Employees
You can enable the chosen user type or types to be only blocked, or only deleted, or both blocked and deleted after a certain period of inactivity.
The period spans from 14 up to 2000 days.

To enable blocking and or deletion, you must choose at least one user type and set at least one period.

After a user has exceeded the set period of inactivity, they will be blocked and their status will be updated on the next sign-in attempt.

You can also configure when the deletion script starts.

Steps to enable 

  1. Sign in to the administration console for SAP Cloud Identity Services.
  2. Under Applications and Resources, choose the Tenant Settings tile.

    At the top of the page, you can view the administrative and license relevant information of the tenant.

  3. Choose the Blocking and Deletion list item.
  4. Choose Edit.
  5. Select a user type.
  6. Enable the options under Inactivity Periods. By default the options are disabled. At least one of the status fields Block after or Delete after must be enabled.
How to find the Last Logon Date of the User to start the Inactivity Blocking User.

User Persona (Examples)

By configuring a 90-day inactivity rule, organizations can automatically block users who have not accessed SAP applications, allowing IT administrators and application owners to quickly identify dormant accounts and strengthen overall security governance.
Mixed User Persona for Block & Deletion
 

By tailoring inactivity rules for different user types, organizations can strike the perfect balance between security, compliance, and user experience—turning identity lifecycle management into a strategic advantage

 

Reference : https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-identity-services/cloud-identity-services/block-or-delete-users-due-to-inactivity?version=Cloud

entry.title: Re: Confused in taking the courses.
entry.content:
Right now your question leaves some gaps. For example, you have not mentioned your educational background, how many years of IT/domain experience you have, or which areas of IT you are strongest in. These details matter because SAP has multiple tracks (technical, functional, and hybrid), and the best starting point depends on your profile.

Here is a practical way forward:

1) Clarify your background
Note your degree, IT specialization (networking, databases, support, etc.), and years of experience.

2) Pick a starting module
If you are more technical → look at SAP Basis/Administration or SAP ABAP (development). If you want functional/business process work → explore SAP MM (Materials Management), SAP SD (Sales & Distribution), or SAP FI (Finance).

3) Courses
Begin with SAP Learning Hub (official) or openSAP free courses to get structured knowledge.

4) Certifications
Once you identify your track, aim for an entry-level certification (e.g., SAP Certified Application Associate in your chosen module).

Try to get access to a training system or sandbox to practice transactions and scenarios.

My advice would be first decide whether you want to go technical (system/development) or functional (business processes), then pick one module and build depth. That focus will make your transition smoother.

Alternatively, you can create a custom report tailored to your needs using the Plateau Report Designer (PRD), leveraging the database tables found in the Learning Data Dictionary.

Relevant KBAs:

  • 2379690 - SuccessFactors Learning - Custom Report Development and Support Scope

entry.title: Re: How To unprotect Excel sheet without password?
entry.content:
If you forgot the password of your Excel sheet, then there are several ways available to unprotect the file without a password. Many users first try manual techniques because they are free and easily available online. Methods like VBA code and changing the Excel file extension can sometimes help remove normal worksheet or workbook protection from Excel files. These approaches are mainly useful when the file only has editing restrictions and not strong encryption.

Some commonly used manual methods include:

  • Using VBA code to bypass worksheet protection
  • Changing XLSX file extension to ZIP and editing internal XML files
  • Saving the workbook in another format and reopening it
  • Trying previously used or partially remembered passwords
  • Using Microsoft-recommended recovery options

Although these methods may work in some situations, they also have many limitations. VBA scripts usually fail in newer Excel versions, and changing the file format manually can be confusing for non-technical users. In some cases, incorrect editing may even corrupt the Excel workbook. Most importantly, these tricks cannot remove “Password to Open” security because modern Excel files use strong AES encryption technology.

If your Excel file is encrypted with a complex password, then manual methods are usually not enough. In such cases, using a professional solution becomes necessary. SysTools Excel Password Recovery Tool  is one of the most reliable solutions to recover lost or forgotten Excel passwords legally and securely. The software supports both old and new Excel file formats, including XLS and XLSX.

Key highlights of the tool:

  • Recover lost or forgotten Excel passwords smoothly
  • Supports workbook and worksheet password recovery
  • Can recover “Password to Open” protected files
  • Works with AES encrypted Excel files
  • Supports unknown and partially known passwords
  • Maintains complete data integrity
  • Compatible with multiple Microsoft Excel versions
  • No advanced technical knowledge required
  • Fast and reliable password recovery process

Therefore, if basic manual methods fail to unprotect your Excel sheet, then using a trusted professional solution is the safest option to recover the password without risking your important data.

The custom Situation Object creation available on SAP On Premise 2025 version. You can check the documentation on SAP Help press F1.

For test purpose, you can use SAP Object Type  'BusinessSituation'.

My custom Situation Handling is working, as I mentioned before i created ABAP objects from scratch ( Z Table, Z CDS, Z Class, etc. )

FYI I use On Premise 2023 Version.

Another thing is you have to add C1 contract on your CDS and set them as 'Release' so you can assign them on the Situation Object's configuration. Right click on your CDS from ADT -> Show in -> Properties 

Hope it helps your exploration.

Regards,

Yoppie A.

entry.title: How to handle payment workflow in Public Cloud without J78 activation?
entry.content:
Hi SAP,

How to handle payment workflow in Public Cloud without J78 activation?

Best regards,


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to handle payment workflow in Public Cloud withou...
entry.content:
Hi,

Thank you for raising this question in SAP Community.

To enable approval workflow for payment runs in the Manage Automatic Payments app in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, there are two prerequisites that must be in place:

  • Activation of the Scope Advanced Cash Operations (J78) is required.
  • In the configuration activity Define Basic Settings (SSCUI ID 101025) cash scope must be selected as Full Scope.

At this time, there is no way to enable this functionality without the activation of the scope item J78. If approval workflow for payment runs is needed in your landscape, please consider this option.

For more details regarding payment approval workflow, see KBA 3731235 - Enabling approval workflow for payment runs in Manage Automatic Payments app in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - SAP for Me.

Thank you for your understanding.

entry.title: Request for Insights: SuccessFactors RCM vs SmartRecruiters for PSU Recruiting
entry.content:
Dear All,

I would like to initiate a discussion on identifying the most suitable recruiting solution for Indian PSU implementations—specifically evaluating SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting Management (RCM) versus SmartRecruiters.

Considering the complexity and regulatory nature of PSU recruitment processes—such as reservation policies, audit requirements, large-scale hiring, and compliance-driven workflows—it is important that we assess which platform aligns best with these needs.

Additionally, with ongoing and upcoming innovations expected to be focused more on the SmartRecruiters platform, it becomes even more relevant to evaluate its suitability in comparison to RCM in the PSU context.

I request everyone to share their insights, experiences, and recommendations to help us arrive at a well-informed approach.

Looking forward to your valuable inputs.

Best regards,
Aravind
